title: limodorum athense
intro: Limodorum athense, also known as the adder's tongue orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid that grows in woodland environments. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Limodorum athense are thought to include the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of Limodorum athense typically involve drying the roots and rhizomes, then using them to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
